free_text: Measurement of double-differential neutron emission
cross sections of Mo, Ta and W
Main reference,
Graphs are given
Experimental details
Dept. of Nuclear Engeneering
4.5 MV dynamitron facility of Tohoku
Univ.
Pulsed neutron beam less than 2 nsec duration 1 MHz
repetition bombarded tritium loaded metallic Ti
target
Single-TOF method was used
Liquid NE123 scin detector 14 cm diameter
and 10 cm thickness coupled to fast-timing
photomultiplier tube
For low energy region
Corrections were made on detectors efficiency and
sample-out background, on finite sample and back-
grounds caused by parasitic and target-scattered
neutrons
Standard deviation
Data obtained from M. Baba, 1997/06.
+ +  Compiled by S.M.     + +
Reactions corrected. Convertewd to new
date formats, lower case.
Date of REFERENCE NST,27 corrected
SD: Year of the JAERI-M-93-046,345 ref.
.No information
Data corrected by authors in 1992
Reaction corrected.
.Metallic cylinder of natural molybdenum  3 cm diameter
and 5 cm long suspended vertically at a distance of
12 cm from the neutron source .
File DAT-MO.DDX
Reaction corrected.
.Metallic cylinder of natural molybdenum  3 cm diameter
and 5 cm long suspended vertically at a distance of
12 cm from the neutron source
The laboratory double differential data
corrected for sample-dependent effects were transformed
into the center-of-mass system assuming two body
reaction kinematics to derive energy and angular
distributions in CMS. The energy distributions were
obtained by fitting the DDX in CMS with the Legendre
polynomials. The present energy distributions for the
elastic-scattering do not include the contributions
from the angles between 0 deg and 25 deg because of too
steep angular distributions to make angle-integration.
data are presented on Fig.6b left of
reference J,NST,31,(8),757,1994.
Metallic cylinder of natural tungsten 3 cm diameter and
5 cm long suspended vertically at a distance of
12 cm from the neutron source .

File DAT-TA.DDX
Reaction corrected.
Metallic cylinder of natural tantalum 3 cm diameter and
5 cm long suspended vertically at a distance of
12 cm from the neutron source
The laboratory double differential data DDX
corrected for sample-dependent effects were transformed
into the center-of-mass system assuming two body
reaction kinematics to derive energy and angular
distributions in CMS. The energy distributions were
obtained by fitting the DDX in CMS with the Legendre
polynomials. The present energy distributions for the
elastic-scattering do not include the contributions
from the angles between 0 deg and 25 deg because of too
steep angular distributions to make angle-integration.
Data are presented of Fig.6b middle of
reference J,NST,31,(8),757,1994 .
